semiconductor device is disclosed herein including creating a photoresist mixture that includes a surfactant, and a base solvent; one or more boiling point modifying solvents having a boiling point higher in temperature than the base solvent; and one or more hydrophilicity modifying solvents that are more hydrophilic than the base solvent; depositing the photoresist mixture onto a substrate comprising a plurality of UBMLs using a wet film process; and performing a pre-bake process to cure the photoresist mixture.

TW202232480A: Memory devices are disclosed that support multiple power ramping sequences or modes. For example, a level shifter device is operably connected to a memory macro in a memory device. The level shifter device receives at least one gating signal. Based on a state of the at least one gating signal, the level shifter device outputs one or more signals that cause or control voltage signals in or received by the memory macro to ramp up, ramp down, or ramp up and ramp down according to one or more power ramping modes.
TWI791375B: Memory devices are disclosed that support multiple power ramping sequences or modes. For example, a level shifter device is operably connected to a memory macro in a memory device. TW202322135A: Systems, methods, and apparatus including computer-readable mediums for determining read voltages for memory systems are provided. In one aspect, a memory system includes a memory storing data and a memory controller coupled to the memory. The memory controller is configured to: obtain a first reading output of target memory data in the memory using a first read voltage, and in response to determining that the first reading output fails to pass an Error-Correcting Code (ECC) test, provide the first read voltage to the memory. The memory is configured to: determine a second read voltage based on the first read voltage and generate a second reading output of the target memory data using the second read voltage.

determining that the first reading output fails to pass an Error-Correcting Code (ECC) test, provide the first read voltage to the memory. The memory is configured to: determine a second read voltage based on the first read voltage and generate a second reading output of the target memory data using the second read voltage.
TW202309911A: An OTP memory cell is provided. The OTP memory cell includes: an antifuse transistor, wherein a gate terminal of the antifuse transistor is connected to a first word line having a first signal, and the antifuse transistor is selectable between a first state and a second state in response to the first signal; and a selection transistor connected between the antifuse transistor and a bit line, wherein a gate terminal of the selection transistor is connected to a second word line having a second signal, and the selection transistor is configured to provide access to the antifuse transistor in response to the second signal. A first terminal of the antifuse transistor is a vacancy terminal, and a second terminal of the antifuse transistor is connected to the selection transistor.
TWI831108B: A method for manufacturing a semiconductor device includes forming a resist structure including forming a resist layer including a resist composition over a substrate. After forming the resist layer, the resist layer is treated with an additive. The additive is one or more selected from the group consisting of a radical inhibitor, a thermal radical initiator, and a photo radical initiator.
TWI792830B: The present invention provides a clock signal generating circuit including a global phased-locked loop (PLL) and a plurality of local PLLs. In the operation of the clock signal generating circuit, the global PLL is configured to receive a reference clock signal to generate a synchronization clock signal, and the local PLLs are configured to receive the synchronization clock signal to generate a plurality of clock signals, respectively, and the plurality of clock signals are used to generate a plurality of output clock signals.
TW202329145A: A multi-channel memory device includes N first memory blocks, a first redundancy memory block and N first interface circuits. Each of the first interface circuits is coupled to two of the first memory blocks and the first redundancy memory block. The first interface circuits respectively select N first selected memory blocks in the first memory block and the first redundancy memory block according to a plurality of first selection signals. Wherein, N is a positive integer larger than 1.
TW202327369A: A media streaming device includes a power manager, a stream processor, and a voltage detector. The power manager receives a power signal from the media playback device to supply power to the stream processor. The stream processor provides media stream to the media playback device for playback. The voltage detector is electrically coupled to the stream processor and captures at least a part of the power supply current to the stream processor . The stream processor is configured to determine whether the power supply voltage remains stable. When the supply voltage remains stable, the stream processor operates in a first mode to provide media stream. When the power supply voltage is unstable, the stream processor operates in a second mode to provide media stream, and the power consumption of the stream processor in the second mode is lower than the power consumption in the first mode.
TWI792821B: A media streaming device includes a power manager, a stream processor, and a voltage detector. The power manager receives a power signal from the media playback device to supply power to the stream processor. The stream processor provides media stream to the media playback device for playback. The voltage detector is electrically coupled to the stream processor and captures at least a part of the power supply current to the stream processor . The stream processor is configured to determine whether the power supply voltage remains stable. When the supply voltage remains stable, the stream processor operates in a first mode to provide media stream. When the power supply voltage is unstable, the stream processor operates in a second mode to provide media stream, and the power consumption of the stream processor in the second mode is lower than the power consumption in the first mode.
TW202312089A: An image processing device is provided, which includes a low-resolution image capture circuit and a processor. The image capture circuit is configured to capture an image. The processor is connected to the image capture circuit and executes a super resolution model, wherein the super resolution model includes multiple neural network blocks, and the processor is configured to perform the following operations: generating a super-resolution image from the low-resolution image by multiple neural network blocks, where one of the multiple neural network blocks includes a spatial attention model and a channel attention model, the channel attention model is concatenated after the spatial attention model, and the spatial attention model and the channel attention model are configured to enhance weights of a region of interest in the low-resolution image in the super-resolution image. In addition, an image processing method is also disclosed herein.
TWI788172B: An image processing device is provided, which includes a low-resolution image capture circuit and a processor. The image capture circuit is configured to capture an image. The processor is connected to the image capture circuit and executes a super resolution model, wherein the super resolution model includes multiple neural network blocks, and the processor is configured to perform the following operations: generating a super-resolution image from the low-resolution image by multiple neural network blocks, where one of the multiple neural network blocks includes a spatial attention model and a channel attention model, the channel attention model is concatenated after the spatial attention model, and the spatial attention model and the channel attention model are configured to enhance weights of a region of interest in the low-resolution image in the super-resolution image. In addition, an image processing method is also disclosed herein.
TW202327340A: A control circuit is provided. The control circuit is disposed in a first electronic device, and the first electronic device is electrically connected to a second electronic device through an HDMI (High-Definition Multimedia Interface) bus. The control circuit includes: a flash memory comprising an application region and an in-system programming (ISP) region; and a computation unit. When the first electronic device has received an HDMI-CEC (Consumer Electronic Control) command, the computation unit determines whether the HDMI-CEC command satisfies a specific condition. In response to the HDMI-CEC command satisfying the specific condition, the control circuit controls the first electronic device to enter an in-system programming mode to update firmware in the application region or the ISP region.

TWI796906B: A light curing device includes a supporting platform, a photomask and an ultraviolet light source. The supporting platform is configured to support a display device. The photomask is arranged above the display device, and the photomask includes a central area and a peripheral area. The ultraviolet light source is configured to emit ultraviolet light to pass through the photomask to the display device, thereby curing the optical cement. The light transmittance of the central area is less than the light transmittance of the peripheral area, so that the hardness of the optical cement corresponding to the peripheral area is greater than the hardness of the optical cement corresponding to the central area.
TW202326296A: A light curing device includes a supporting platform, a photomask and an ultraviolet light source. The supporting platform is configured to support a display device. TW202326659A: A display apparatus is provided, which includes a display panel and a display controller. The display controller is configured to receive a video signal from a host, and displays the video signal on the display panel. The display controller calculates an image average brightness of each image in the video signal. When an absolute different between a first image average brightness of a current image in the video signal and a second image average brightness of a previous in the video signal is less than a predetermined value, the display controller activates a timer to obtain a timer value. When the timer value reaches a first time period, the display controller lowers brightness of each pixel in the current image by a first ratio. When the timer value reaches a second timer period, the display controller lowers brightness of each pixel in the current image by a second ratio, wherein the second time period is longer than the first time period, and the second ratio is higher than the first ratio.
TWI789174B: A display apparatus is provided, which includes a display panel and a display controller. TW202326745A: An electronic test system includes a reader, a memory, and a processor. The reader is configured to read and write a plurality of data. The memory is configured to store a plurality of commands. The processor is configured to obtain a plurality of commands from the memory to perform the following steps: generating the first random sequence data by the processor; writing the first random sequence data by the reader; reading the read value by the reader, and confirming by the processor whether the read value is the same as the first random sequence data; when the read value is the same as the first random sequence data, the processor generates the second random sequence data, wherein the first random sequence data and the second random sequence data do not overlap; and when the read value is different from the first random sequence data, the first random sequence data is output by the reader.
TWI771252B: An electronic test system includes a reader, a memory, and a processor. The reader is configured to read and write a plurality of data. TW202326591A: A graphic resource allocation method and a graphic resource allocation system are disclosed. TW202326743A: A read disturb checking method, a memory storage device and a memory control circuit unit are provided. TWI796882B: A read disturb checking method, a memory storage device and a memory control circuit unit are provided. TW202326698A: A multi-screen display device and an operation method thereof are provided. The multi-screen display device includes a first screen, a second screen, a displacement sensor, and a processing circuit. The second screen is slid on the first screen to change a display range of the multi-screen display device. The displacement sensor is used to detect a relative position of the second screen to the first screen, and output at least one sensing signal related to the relative position. The processing circuit is coupled to the first screen, the second screen and the displacement sensor. The processing circuit adjusts a display resolution of the second screen according to the at least one sensing signal.
TW202244625A: A reticle is pre-heated prior to an exposure operation of a semiconductor substrate lot to reduce substrate to substrate temperature variations of the reticle in the exposure operation. The reticle may be pre-heated while being stored in a reticle storage slot, while being transferred from the reticle storage slot to a reticle stage of an exposure tool, and/or in another location prior to being secured to the reticle stage for the exposure operation. In this way, the reduction in temperature variation of the reticle in the exposure operation provided by pre-heating the reticle may reduce overlay deltas and misalignment for the semiconductor substrates that are processed in the exposure operation. This increases overlay performance, increases yield of the exposure tool, and increases semiconductor device quality. Moreover, pre-heating the reticle prior to securing the reticle to the reticle stage for the exposure operation reduces and/or minimizes the impact that the pre-heating has on throughput and processing times of the exposure tool.
TW202242568A: A method for forming a semiconductor device is provided. The method for forming a semiconductor device is provided. The method includes coating a photoresist film over a target layer; performing a lithography process to pattern the photoresist film into a photoresist layer; performing a directional ion bombardment process to the photoresist layer, such that a carbon atomic concentration in the photoresist layer is increased; and etching the target layer using the photoresist layer as an etch mask.
TW202327343A: A conference device includes an image capturing unit, a communication unit and a processor. The communication unit communicatively connects to a server and a first electronic device located in the same space with the conference device. The processor is configured to perform an online conference program and process an audio-visual signal received from the server via the communication unit. The processor is configured to transmit certification information of the first electronic device to obtain a cursor operation authority. The processor is configured to receive a real-time visual signal comprising a real-time state generated by applying an operation instruction message matching the cursor operation authority to a representation cursor of the certification information and comprising a real-time state generated by applying the operation instruction message to a representation cursor represented a remote participant of the online conference.
TWI809604B: A conference device includes an image capturing unit, a communication unit and a processor. TW202325008A: A method for automatically switching on/off sound receiving channel of video conference and an electronic device are provided. The method for automatically switching on/off sound receiving channel of video conference includes the following steps. First, a volume information of an electronic is detected. If the volume information is greater than a threshold condition, a video image of a participant participating in the video conference of the electronic device is received. Then, whether the participant is in a speaking state is determined according to the video image. TWI771243B: A memory control method, a memory storage device and a memory control circuit unit are disclosed. TW202244621A: Some implementations described herein provide an exposure tool and associated methods of operation in which a scanner control system generates a scanner route for an exposure recipe such that the distance traveled by a substrate stage of the exposure tool along the scanner route is reduced and/or optimized for non-exposure fields on a semiconductor substrate. TW202324417A: A solid state disk, a data transmitting method and an intermediary controller thereof are provided. TW202322113A: A memory device and an operation method thereof are disclosed. TWI786947B: The present application provides an anti-fuse one-time programmable (OTP) memory array and a manufacturing method of the anti-fuse one-time programmable (OTP) memory array. A region in which a pair of programming word line and read word line are intersected with one of the active areas defines a unit cell in the memory array. TW202234170A: The present disclosure provides a dual developing method for defining different resist patterns. TW202320076A: A memory time-division control device controls a content addressable memory (CAM) cell array of a CAM in a time-division manner to reduce a peak current and mitigate the problems of electromigration and voltage variation. TW202318881A: The present invention is a method for controlling augmented reality content playback and system thereof. A paragraph description file for the content of the augmented reality video is configured in the augmented reality video to generate each paragraph of the film and its audio-visual characteristics. When the augmented reality video is played by an electronic device as the augmented reality content, whether the scene situation meets the recorded characteristics can be compared, and compare results can be used for controlling the playback progress of the content of the augmented reality video. In addition, recording changes in the playback progress due to control actions can also be used as a reference for video content adjustment. Therefore, the present invention allows the user to focus on practical operations without distracting control of the playback progress of the augmented reality video, so that better augmented reality learning operations and efficiency are provided. The present invention also provides a computer-readable medium for executing the method of the present invention.

The second gate structure includes a second insulation layer and a second conductive layer. The first metal layer is connected with the first doped region.

TW202318867A: The disclosure provides an eye tracking method and an eye tracking device. The method includes: obtaining a reference interpupillary distance value; taking images of a user of a 3D display, and finding a first eye pixel coordinate corresponding to a first eye of the user and a second eye pixel coordinate corresponding to a second eye of the user in each image; detecting a first and a second eye spatial coordinates of the first and the second eyes, and determining projection coordinates based on the first eye spatial coordinate, the second eye spatial coordinate, and optical parameters of image capturing elements; determining an optimization condition related to the first and second eye spatial coordinates based on the first and second eye pixel coordinates, the projection coordinates, and the reference interpupillary distance value of each image; and optimizing the first and second eye spatial coordinates based on the optimization condition.
TWI826844B: A method for maintaining integrity of data image, which provides the capability to maintain integrity of a stored data image by computing a hash value (“digest”) of the data image, and by comparing the hash value computed for the data image with a hash value kept in a non-volatile area of memory. TW202240590A: A timing calibration device suitable for an extended data output mode of flash memory is disclosed. The timing control register stores the read delay time of the data read command. When the read delay time controller is in the extended data output mode, it instructs the flash memory controller to read the second test data of the flash memory according to the data read command and the read delay time, and to verify the first test whether the data is the same as the second test data. TWI762257B:
The present invention is a method for recommending the viewing directions in a virtual reality video. During the process of playing a video information on a virtual reality playback device and displaying the virtual reality video by a head-mounted display. A recommended angle from the video information and a viewing angle from the head-mounted display are obtained by the virtual reality playback device. A recommended direction is calculated after comparing the recommended angle and the viewing angle, and the recommended direction 
is displayed in the virtual reality video. Therefore, the present invention can prompt the direction of the recommended screen when the user watches the virtual reality video, thereby quickly and precisely providing the user with a better viewing effect. A computer-readable medium for implementing a method for recommending viewing directions in a virtual reality movie is further provided in the present invention.

TW202136912A: A method of manufacturing semiconductor device includes forming a multilayer photoresist structure over a substrate. The multilayer photoresist structure includes two or more metal-containing photoresist layers having different physical parameters. The metal-containing photoresist is a reaction product of a first precursor and a second precursor, and each layer of the multilayer photoresist structure is formed using different photoresist layer formation parameters. The photoresist layer formation parameters are one or more selected from the group consisting of the first precursor, an amount of the first precursor, the second precursor, an amount of the second precursor, a length of time each photoresist layer formation operation, and heating conditions of the photoresist layers. The multilayer photoresist structure is selectively exposed to actinic radiation to form a latent pattern, and the latent pattern is developed by applying developer to the selectively exposed multilayer photoresist structure to form the pattern.

TWI779523B: An electric device having OTP memory and method for writing and reading the OTP memory thereof is provided. The electric device is adapted to execute a method for writing and reading an one-time-programmable (OTP) memory. The electric device comprises an OTP memory, an internal memory and a processor. The processor writes an original data into the OTP memory as a written data. The processor reads the written data from the OTP memory. When the written data is not identical to the original data, the processor generates a correction code according to the original data, the written data and a correction procedure, and stores the correction code into the internal memory. Upon receipt of a reading request, the processor generates the original data according to the written data, the correction code and a recovery procedure.

TW202141160A: A method of fabricating a semiconductor device includes providing a first substrate and forming a resist layer over the first substrate. In some embodiments, the method further includes performing an exposure process to the resist layer. In some examples, the intervening mask includes a second substrate, a multi-layer structure formed over the second substrate, a capping layer formed over the multi-layer structure, and an absorber layer disposed over the capping layer. In some embodiments, the absorber layer includes a first main pattern area and an opening area spaced a distance from the first main pattern area. In various examples, the method further includes, after performing the exposure process, developing the exposed resist layer to form a patterned resist layer.

The word lines include a first word line and a second word line that are programmed and not adjacent to each other. The operation method includes the following steps. A read voltage is applied to a selected word line. A pass voltage is applied to unselected word lines, and the read voltage is less than the pass voltage. TWI766559B:
An operation method of a memory device is provided. The memory device includes a P-type well, a common source line, a memory array, a plurality of word lines, and a serial selection line, a 
ground selection line, and at least one bit line. The word lines include a first word line and a second word line that are programmed and not adjacent to each other. The operation method includes the following steps. A read voltage is applied to a selected word line. A pass voltage is applied to unselected word lines, and the read voltage is less than the pass voltage. During a period when the pass voltage ramps down to a lower level before the end of a read operation, a channel potential of the memory string is down-coupled, a hole current is injected to flow from the P-type well to the memory string to neutralize the channel voltage in advance.
TW202142964A: A method of manufacturing a semiconductor device includes forming a photoresist under-layer including a photoresist under-layer composition over a semiconductor substrate, and forming a photoresist layer including a photoresist composition over the photoresist under-layer. The photoresist layer is selectively exposed to actinic radiation and the photoresist layer is developed to form a pattern in the photoresist layer. The photoresist under-layer composition includes a polymer having pendant acid-labile groups, a polymer having crosslinking groups or a polymer having pendant carboxylic acid groups, an acid generator, and a solvent. The photoresist composition includes a polymer, a photoactive compound, and a solvent.

TWI757054B:
The present invention provides a receiver including a sampling circuit, a data sampling point selection circuit and a determination circuit. The sampling circuit is configured to use a clock signal to sample an input signal to generate a sampled signal, wherein a frequency of the clock signal is higher than a frequency of the input signal. The data sampling point selection circuit is configured to filter start point data to generate a filtered start point data, and to generate a data sampling point by adding an offset to the filtered start point data, wherein the start point data corresponds to a time point that a sampled value of sampled signal starts to change. The determination 
circuit is configured to refer to a sampled value corresponding to the data sampling point in the sampled signal to determine a logical value of a digital output signal corresponding to the input signal.

TWI746336B: A Storage device management method for a storage device is provided. TW202137184A: A display device and a driving protection method thereof are provided. The display device includes a timing controller and a source driver. TWI759988B:
Various embodiments of the present disclosure are directed 
towards a method for forming a semiconductor structure and a processing system. The method includes forming a plurality of upper alignment marks on a semiconductor wafer. A plurality of lower alignment marks is formed on a handle wafer and correspond to the upper alignment marks. The semiconductor wafer is bonded to the handle wafer such that centers of the upper alignment marks are laterally offset from centers of corresponding lower alignment 
marks. An overlay (OVL) shift is measured between the handle wafer and the semiconductor wafer by detecting the plurality of upper alignment marks and the plurality of lower alignment marks. A photolithography process is performed by a photolithography tool to partially form an integrated circuit (IC) structure over the semiconductor wafer. During the photolithography process the photolithography tool is compensatively aligned according to the OVL shift.

An overlay (OVL) shift is measured between the handle wafer and the semiconductor wafer by detecting the plurality of upper alignment marks and the plurality of lower alignment marks. A photolithography process is performed by a photolithography tool to partially form an integrated circuit (IC) structure over the semiconductor wafer. During the photolithography process the photolithography tool is compensatively aligned according to the OVL shift.
TWI772988B: A system for verifying integrity of content of an integrated circuit's registers, the system being operative in conjunction with an integrated circuit including at least one memory, at least one processor, and a multiplicity of registers, the system comprising register content verification logic configured, when in a first mode aka " Study Mode", to read at least some of the registers' content, to compute a first hash on the content, and to store the first hash thereby to provide an up-to-date reference hash, and, at least on occasion, when in a second mode aka " Verify Mode ", to compute at least one second hash on the content, to compare the second hash to the reference hash and, accordingly, to provide a content verification output (aka "fault detection" output) indicative of whether the reference and second hashes are equal.

TW202221684A: A pixel circuit includes a current source circuit, a light-emitting element and a switch circuit. The current source circuit provides a drive current. TW202113458A: The present disclosure describes a method of patterning a semiconductor wafer using extreme ultraviolet lithography (EUVL). The method includes receiving an EUVL mask that includes a substrate having a low temperature expansion material, a reflective multilayer over the substrate, a capping layer over the reflective multilayer, and an absorber layer over the capping layer. The method further includes patterning the absorber layer to form a trench on the EUVL mask, wherein the trench has a first width above a target width. The method further includes treating the EUVL mask with oxygen plasma to reduce the trench to a second width, wherein the second width is below the target width. The method may also include treating the EUVL mask with nitrogen plasma to protect the capping layer, wherein the treating of the EUVL mask with the nitrogen plasma expands the trench to a third width at the target width.

baking process to the MePR layer and the BARC layer, thereby reacting a metal chemical structure of the MePR layer and the reactive chemical structure of the BARC layer and forming an interface layer between the MePR layer and the BARC layer; performing an exposure process using the EUV radiation to the MePR layer; and developing the MePR layer to form a patterned photoresist layer.

TW202107466A:
In some embodiments, the present disclosure relates to a method of operating a phase change memory cell, which includes writing a first data state and a second data state to the phase change memory cell. To write the first data state, a phase change material (PCM) is heated to a melting point of the PCM, and then cooled to an ambient temperature below the melting point of the PCM over a first predetermined time period, thereby solidifying the PCM to correspond to the first data state. To write the second data state, the PCM is heated to the melting point of the PCM, and then cooled to the ambient temperature over a second predetermined cooling time period, thereby solidifying the PCM to correspond to the second data state. The second predetermined cooling time period differs from the first predetermined time period
.

TW202109175A: A method for fabricating a photomask is provided. The method includes several operations. TWI726622B: A semiconductor device capable of suppressing generation of noise caused by EMI is provided. The clock generator (200) generates an internal clock signal (InCLK). The readout part reads data from a selected memory cell of the memory cell array (110) using the internal clock signal (InCLK). The input/output circuit (150) outputs the read data using an external clock signal (ExCLK) supplied from outside. The overlap detecting unit (330) detects a period during which a rising edge of the internal clock signal (InCLK) overlaps with a rising edge of the external clock signal (ExCLK). The clock control part (210) controls a timing of the internal clock signal (InCLK) in response to the detected overlap period.

TW202032554A: A pseudo static random access memory including a memory controller, a plurality of memory chips and an information storing device is provided. The fixed period is larger than an initial latency. The information storing device is configured to store an information which defines the fixed period. The read/write data strobe signal indicates whether the self refresh collision happens in the memory chips, and a level of the read/write data strobe signal is constant during the read latency. A method for operating a pseudo static random access memory is also provided.

TW202127144A: A method for fabricating a semiconductor device includes steps as follows: First, a substrate is provided and a silicon-containing anti-reflection material is coated on the substrate. A photoresist layer is formed on the anti-reflection layer , and an exposure process is performed on the photoresist layer to convert a portion of the photoresist layer into a sacrificial region. The remaining antireflection layer is removed by a first solvent.

TW202029063A: A system includes an accelerator to accelerate the computations of nonlinear math functions. The accelerator includes a set of first evaluators and a set of second evaluators. Each of the first evaluators and the second evaluators is a fixed-function hardware circuit specialized for evaluating a respective nonlinear function. The system further includes a processor which decodes a math instruction in an instruction set, sends to the accelerator a signal identifying a nonlinear function corresponding to the math instruction, and receives an output of the nonlinear function from the accelerator. TWI732410B:
This invention provides a system and method for automatic reporting of detecting a set-top box abnormally, includes a detector with abnormal 
control module, Log storage module, and network connection module, and also a server with corresponded network connection module, abnormal control module, Log storage module, database, analysis module, searching interface, abnormal message generated module, and network transfer module. When a detector detects a set-top box occurring abnormal problem, the abnormal message can be caught in time and transfer to the server. After checking the integrity of the abnormal message to be analyzed, the server will send the abnormal message to related crews, or feedback the solution of the abnormal message directly. The system and method for automatic reporting of detecting a set-top box abnormally can automatically and immediately transfer the solution of the abnormal message if the abnormal message is known, or transfer to the related crews to handle. This can make the related crew realize the problem with key points and solutions thereof before the customer responds the problem, and further improve the efficiency of solving the abnormal message and the quality of the set-top box.

TWI701674B: A memory apparatus and a data reading method thereof are provided. TW202126052A: The disclosure provides a Game Raiders video recommendation system, a raider providing device and method thereof, which adapt to communicating with a data server. The data server searches for at least one video raider information according to the video feature, and the processing module generates the video raider information list based on the video raider information. The processing module selects one from the video raider information list according to the control signal of the input device, and the corresponding one in the selected video raider information list is a raider image to be displayed, and finally displays the video raider information list or the raider image to be displayed. TW202036505A: A power saving method applied to source drivers of a display is disclosed. The method includes steps of: grouping the output channels of each source driver respectively; setting a charge-sharing average register; selecting whether the data signal is reverse processed; calculating an average of the data lines after charge-sharing; calculating the total power consumption of the data signal from the (N-1)-th data line to the N-th data line under all charge-sharing modes, wherein N is a positive integer greater than 1; selecting a candidate lowest power consumption charge-sharing mode corresponding to each source driver; voting the lowest power charge-sharing mode from those candidate lowest power consumption charge-sharing modes; and controlling the operation of each source driver according to the lowest power charge-sharing mode.
TW202123094A: The present invention is a method for improving the accuracy of classification problems in neural networks and computer readable storage medium. The method deals with complex nonlinear problems through the characteristics of cubic function. The characteristic is that when the input value is between ±2, the output value changes little, and if the input value exceeds this range, as the value increases or decreases, the increase or decrease of the output value will have a significant difference. The result is passed to the next neuron or the output layer, the next neuron or output will be more significant. Therefore, the present invention improve the passivation problem of prior art, and can achieve higher accuracy faster in fewer training times, and can avoid the problem of the gradient vanishing that may occur when the hidden layer of the neural network is increased. Further, the whole effectiveness and performance could be improved.

TWM677294U: This invention relates to a server with remote management capabilities, comprising a motherboard, a processor module, an I/O module, and a BMC module. According to this design, the I/O module can transmit data with the processor module based on the PCIe protocol, thereby enabling in-band management of the server. Furthermore, the I/O module can also transmit data with the BMC module based on the NCSI protocol, thereby enabling out-of-band management of the server. With this design, when a network acceleration platform (such as NVIDIA BlueField-3) is added to the server, the BF-3 can be dedicated to processing and forwarding massive amounts of data, eliminating the need for system administrators to perform in-band management of the server through the BF-3. In this way, when system administrators perform remote in-band management of the server, it will not consume or affect the bandwidth of the BF-3's main data channel. Ultimately, this new type of server provides system administrators, maintainers, or developers with more flexible server management operations when building environments or systems that include multiple servers.

The processor is coupled to the communication circuit and configured to execute: in response to determining that a version of a first update file from an update server satisfies a preset condition, controlling the communication circuit to obtain the first update file from the update server; reading identification information from at least one predetermined data location of the first update file; in response to determining that the identification information in the first update file matches the network device, updating the network device based on the first update file.
TW202437736A:
The invention discloses a multiple terminal end-to-end encryption communication method and computer readable medium. A sending terminal generates a temporary key pair with a temporary public key and a temporary private key for a receiving account, and then calculates an account public key and the temporary private key to generate a ratchet key. Next, the sending terminal uses the ratchet key to derive a message key, and uses the message key 
to encrypt a message to generate a message ciphertext, and then uploads the message ciphertext and key information to a server. Thereafter, the server sends the message ciphertext and the key information to multiple receiving terminals. When the receiving terminal receives the message ciphertext, the receiving terminal uses the key information to generate the message key, and then uses the message key to decrypt the message from the message ciphertext.
TW202435592A: A job processing method based on a cloud service includes: receiving a job request and obtaining an identification string of the job according to the job request by a job scheduler, sending the identification string to a memory cache and generating a worker by the job scheduler, obtains the identification string from the memory cache and obtaining data from a database according to the identification string by the worker, and executing the job based on the data and sending a result of the job to the database by the worker.
TWI873540B: A job processing method based on a cloud service includes: receiving a job request and obtaining an identification string of the job according to the job request by a job scheduler, sending the identification string to a memory cache and generating a worker by the job scheduler, obtains the identification string from the memory cache and obtaining data from a database according to the identification string by the worker, and executing the job based on the data and sending a result of the job to the database by the worker.
TW202433901A:
The present invention is an automatic certificate application system and method thereof. The automatic certificate application system includes a certificate management module, a domain verification module and a certification authority module. The certificate management module is configured to confirm and register an automated service account. The domain verification module is configured to adopt a corresponding domain verification 
method according to a domain verification application packet sent by the certificate management module. The certification authority module is configured to decide whether to issue certificates based on domain verification results. Moreover, the domain verification method has a corresponding object identifier. Because the object identifier is bound with the relevant information of domain verification, it can provide a restriction mechanism for the domain verification method adopted, and the information security problem of the domain verification can be avoided. The present invention also provides a computer-readable medium for executing the method of the present invention.
TW202433900A: 一種基於行動網路切片功能之網路功能虛擬化編排系統、方法及其電腦可讀媒介
TW202433892A: An agent authorization system and an agent authorization method using NFT technology are provided. The method includes: creating a power-of-attorney-NFT-token by a power-of-attorney-NFT-token-setting-element of a client device, and writing the power-of-attorney-NFT-token into a first block of a blockchain; writing the power-of-attorney-NFT-token into a second block of the blockchain by a power-of-attorney-NFT-token-proxy-authorization-element of an agent device; and creating a certification-document-NFT-token by a certification-document-NFT-token-setting-element of the certification-document-issuing-unit-device, and writing the certification-document-NFT-token into a third block of the blockchain, wherein the certification-document-NFT-token includes the power-of-attorney-NFT-token-identification-code.
TW202433889A: The present disclosure provides remote authentication physical security key system and method. The remote authentication physical security key system comprises a physical key, a control device and a controlled platform. The control device is electrically connected to the physical key. The controlled platform connects to the control device through a digital transmission. The control device transmits an authentication apply information to an application service program which links to the controlled platform through the digital transmission. The controlled platform responses a challenge signature process through the digital transmission according to the authentication apply information to request a signature. The control device processes a signature process to the physical key according to the challenge signature process to complete authentication. Therefore, the control device authenticates the physical key by remote. The purpose of rising an information safety may be achieved.
TW202429866A: A clound native Virtualised Network Function (VNF) management system and method, the method includes, determining whether VNF or the VNF in a Network Service(NS) is a clound native network function(CNF) according to the NS or the VNF listed by a user, analyzing CNF functional characteristic information of the CNF, and storing the CNF functional characteristic information, orchestrating and managing network functions for each network controller, performing lifecycle management function operations on NS or VNF, and performing abnormal repair according to the CNF functional characteristic information when an abnormality occurs in the NS or the VNF service.
TW202429856A: A system and method for assessing security standard, the system includes a web server to be detected, a database to be detected and a status detector. The status detector is for a user to set server status security standard, server similar function list, database status security standard and database similar function list, the status detector is used to detect the server status of the web server to be detected according to the server status security standard and the server similar function list, perform database status detection on the database to be detected according to the database status security standard and database similar function list, and the state detector is used to carry out state association detection on the web server to be detected and the database to be detected with connection behavior according to the state association similar function list.
TW202427987A: A key management device for avoiding using incomplete keys is provided. The key management device comprises a static random access memory (SRAM), a register and a control circuit. The control circuit arranges a key lookup table in the SRAM or the register, wherein the key database stores one or more keys; wherein the control circuit executes: receiving a key creation instruction sent by a processor, wherein the key creation instruction includes a new key and corresponding metadata; when the new key has been stored in the key database, and the corresponding metadata of the new key has been added in the key lookup table, setting the activation bit corresponding to the new key is ON in the key lookup table; and reporting a key number corresponding to the new key.
TW202425607A: A server and a method for receiving and processing messages applied to multiple services are provided. The method of the present invention is based on a registration notification mode, and proposes mechanisms such as uniqueness of message processing, guarantee of validity period, no loss of messages, and dynamic allocation of processing capabilities that are not available in the existing technologies, so that registration and notification can be performed on different service channels at the same time. Based on this, it is feasible to receive and transmit a large number of messages for multiple services, which is suitable for intermediary applications of message exchange.
TW202425574A: An automatic domain verification system, a certificate issuance method and a computer-readable medium are provided, which perform the automatic verification of the ownership or the right of control of the domain name after receiving a certificate application without manual interaction with the contact window of the applicant, so as to avoid information security problems or certificate mis-issue due to human error.
TW202425600A: The present invention discloses a communication network operation monitoring method, comprising: presenting a command input interface on a terminal device of a first party, wherein the command input interface is used to receive one or more to-be-executed commands inputted from the first party; and having a command detection and blocking module compare the one or more to-be-executed commands with a blacklist to determine whether the system automatically blocks the one or more to-be-executed commands or manually blocks the one or more to-be-executed commands after manual review, wherein the blacklist includes a plurality of keywords related to high-risk commands.
TW202425579A: A virtual machine in cloud service disaster recovery system and method based on distributed storage technology, which is applicable to local cloud service, local cloud storage service, backup cloud service and backup cloud storage service. The method includes receiving off-site backup and restoration instruction and setting the backup virtual machine and backup virtual disk in the backup cloud storage service to readable and writable status; searching backup intermediary data to obtain and send restore data and virtual machine specification information to create a backup virtual machine or backup virtual disk to be restored in the backup zone cloud service; starting the backup virtual machine or backup virtual disk in the backup zone cloud service; checking weather the backup virtual machine or backup virtual disk to be restored start normally; and sending an alarm message according to whether the backup virtual machine or backup virtual disk to be restored is started normally.
TW202423091A: The present invention provides a console management system and method of virtual machine and computer-readable medium thereof including a virtualization management device with a cross-platform interface module and a plurality of hypervisors with a plurality of virtual machines, wherein the virtualization management device provides a user to create a virtual data center in a browser operation interface, and after completing the establishment of the virtual data center, the virtualization management device uses the cross-platform interface module to monitor the virtual machines of each platform In this way, users can quickly and conveniently monitor virtual machines under the hypervisors of each platforms through the virtual data center, so as to greatly improve the management efficiency of virtual machines.
TW202420786A: A monitoring system and a monitoring method for an abnormal behavior of a user equipment are provided. The method includes: obtaining a first network traffic of a first user equipment and generating a domain white list corresponding to the first user equipment according to the first network traffic; obtaining a first current network traffic of the first user equipment and determining a access behavior of the first user equipment to a first domain according to the first current network traffic; in response to the first domain not being in the domain white list, determining whether the first user equipment is abnormal according to the access behavior; and in response to the first user equipment being abnormal, outputting an alarm message.
TW202420795A: The invention provides an interface conversion device and operating method thereof. The interface conversion device comprises a USB connector, a DP connector, a first physical layer (PHY) circuit, a second PHY circuit, a digital buffer, a USB controller and a path switching circuit. The first terminal of the first PHY circuit is coupled to the USB connector. The digital buffer and the USB controller are coupled to the second terminal of the first PHY circuit. The first terminal of the second PHY circuit is coupled to the DP connector. The path switching circuit selectively electrically connects the second terminal of the second PHY circuit to the output terminal of the digital buffer when the interface conversion device operates in a DP ALT Mode. The path switching circuit selectively electrically connects the second terminal of the second PHY circuit to the DP output terminal of the USB controller when the interface conversion device operates in a tunneling mode.
TW202420793A: The invention provides an interface conversion device comprising a USB connector, a DP connector, a first physical layer (PHY) circuit, a second PHY circuit, a digital buffer, a USB controller and a path switching circuit. The first terminal of the first PHY circuit is coupled to the USB connector. The digital buffer and the USB controller are coupled to the second terminal of the first PHY circuit. The first terminal of the second PHY circuit is coupled to the DP connector. The path switching circuit selectively electrically connects the second terminal of the second PHY circuit to the output terminal of the digital buffer when the interface conversion device operates in a DP ALT Mode. The path switching circuit selectively electrically connects the second terminal of the second PHY circuit to the DP output terminal of the USB controller when the interface conversion device operates in a tunneling mode.
TW202418783A: A method of locking a storage device includes connecting the storage device to a host; inputting a communication number to the host; the host sending a unique machine code of the storage device and the communication number to a server; the server encrypting the unique machine code to form an encrypted text message and sending the encrypted text message to a personal mobile communication device corresponding to the communication number; activating the encrypted text message and generating an activation notification through an authentication program of the personal mobile communication device confirming an identity of user; after the server receives the activation notification, the server generating and transmitting a password information to the host and storing the password information and the communication number in a database in a corresponding table form; and the host locking the storage device according to the password information and storing the communication number to the storage device.
TW202415042A: A BMC stress testing method includes the following steps: First, create an automatic test script, and the automatic test script has a plurality of static IP addresses and a plurality of dynamic IP addresses. Then, a test server is used to execute the automatic test script, so that the test server uses a pressure test tool to pressurize a server under test, and when a load value of the server under test reaches a first preset pressure value, and modify a management port of the server to be tested to a dedicated interface to establish an out-of-band channel. Then, the test server uses the static IP addresses and the dynamic IP addresses to perform a connection test with a baseboard management controller of the server to be tested according to the automatic test script, and uses the out-of-band connection channel to check the test results.
TW202415030A: A network card, a power supply control method for the network card, and a server. The network card includes a network card control module, at least one network card interface, a power supply control module and at least one voltage conversion module; the first end of the network card control module is electrically connected with the motherboard controller, and the second end of the network card control module is connected through the network card interface. Connect with the power receiving device; the first end of the power supply control module is connected with the network card interface, and the power supply control module is used to obtain the parameter information of the power receiving device through the network card interface; the input end of the voltage conversion module is connected to the power module of the server motherboard. connection, the control end of the voltage conversion module is connected with the second end of the power supply control module, the output end of the voltage conversion module is connected with the network card interface, and the power supply control module is used to control the voltage conversion when the parameter information conforms to the preset information The module supplies power to the powered device through the network card interface. The technical solutions of the embodiments of the present invention achieve the effect of protecting the power receiving device, and better supply power to the power receiving device through the network card.
TW202412499A: A one-to-many communication circuit includes a recommended standard 232 (RS-232) signal transceiver, a main controller area network (CAN) signal transceiver, and a plurality of slave controller area network signal transceivers. The recommended standard 232 signal transceiver is configured to receive a control signal from the controller and convert the control signal to a first signal that meets the recommended standard 232 protocol. The main controller area network signal transceiver is configured to convert the first signal to a second signal that meets the controller area network protocol. Each of the plurality of slave controller area network signal transceiver is configured to convert the second signal to a third signal that meets the recommended standard 232 signal protocol.
TW202412488A: An avatar carrier generating system for metaverse and method thereof is disclosed. By generating at least one physiological characteristic through at least one sensing element and receiving a real-name identity information before a living body enters a metaverse, and converting the real-name identity information and physiological characteristics into a metadata of a non-fungible token (NFT) standard, and then generating the NFT by the living body on blockchain according to the metadata, so that when the living body wants to enter the metaverse, generating an avatar carrier that enters the metaverse according to the metadata of the NFT, and when the avatar carrier receives an identification request, it is allowed to provide the at least one of physiological characteristic and real-name identity information. The mechanism is help to improve the identifiability and verifiability of the avatar carrier of the metaverse.
TW202410664A: An Over-the-Air (OTA) method includes the following steps. An over-the-air download controller determines whether a download command is received in a power down mode. The over-the-air controller responds to the download command is received in a power down mode, the over-the-air controller determines whether the download command is received. The air download controller sends a notification to a server. A certificate verification module determines whether server certificate is correct, and determines whether server identity is correct. New firmware or new software is received. And, the certificate verification module determines whether the new firmware or the new software has been received. In response to the certificate verification module determining that the new firmware or new software has been received, the certificate verification module determines whether the new firmware or whether the new software is complete and correct.
TW202410665A: An Over-the-Air (OTA) method includes: controlling a current state through a control state register, and recording the current state and a hash algorithm; obtaining data from a server through a sequence setting device after calculating a random number from the calculator, arranging the random number according to a preset sorting method to obtain a sorting parameter; and using the sorting parameter as the input of the hash algorithm by a hash calculator. The hash algorithm outputs a device-side hash result, and transmits the device-side hash result to the server through a transmission device. After receiving the device-side hash result, the server compares the device-side hash result with a server-side hash result calculated by the server.
TW202309898A: The present disclosure describes a method for memory cell placement. The method can include placing a memory cell region in a layout area and placing a well pick-up region and a first power supply routing region along a first side of the memory cell region. The method also includes placing a second power supply routing region and a bitline jumper routing region along a second side of the memory cell region, where the second side is on an opposite side to that of the first side. The method further includes placing a device region along the second side of the memory cell region, where the bitline jumper routing region is between the second power supply routing region and the device region.
TW202402010A: An apparatus for synchronous ethernet comprises a processor, an FPGA (field programmable gate array), a synchronizer, a physical layer executor and a media access controller. The processor transmits control data through a first transmitting interface. The FPGA receives the control data, generates a control instruction in accordance with the control data and transmits the control instruction through a second transmitting interface. The synchronizer receives the control instruction and generates a clock signal in accordance with the control instruction. The physical layer executor receives the clock signal from the synchronizer and receives network data from or transmits network data to the ethernet. The media access controller receives the clock signal from the synchronizer and receives network data from or transmits prepared data to the physical layer executor in accordance with the clock signal and media access control protocol.
TW202349909A: An image encryption system includes a memory and a processor. The memory is configured to store a plurality of commands. The processor is configured to perform the following steps according to the plurality of commands from the memory: adding a text string to an area on an image, and the text string includes a high grayscale text string and a low grayscale text string; during the time period, obtaining an average value of the encrypted high gray level of the high gray level text string and the encrypted low grayscale of the low grayscale text string; and when the average value is equal to a display grayscale of the image, outputting the image and the text string.
TW202339473A: A method for configuring network traffic and a computer system are provided. The computer system includes a processing unit and a network module that is operated based on a bandwidth configuration. The processing unit operates an operating system that executes a profiling-controlling program. In the method for configuring network traffic being operated in the operating system, the operating system initiates a system core that allows the profiling-controlling program to obtain core operation information from the system core. Specifically, the information relating to multiple applications that respectively operate in a foreground process and a background process can be retrieved. A priority is decided and a network bandwidth configuration is formed and written to traffic control instructions or a driver of the network module in the system core. The network bandwidth configuration allows the operating system to perform a traffic configuration to each of the applications.
TW202332229A: 用來進行功耗擾動操作以降低密碼系統功耗分析攻擊的成功率之方法、密碼系統處理電路及電子裝置
TW202339474A: A method for IPv6 information security testing, a corresponding system, and a corresponding computer-readable medium are provided. The method includes: providing at least one device under test (DUT) deployed in an external network domain and/or an internal network domain, performing at least one IPv6 test on the at least one DUT; and validating IPv6 core operation status of the at least one DUT after performing the at least one IPv6 test so as to determine whether the at least one DUT has IPv6 information security vulnerability.
TW202339476A: The present invention discloses a bypass switch device with indicating function for use in a network appliance having at least one Ethernet network module. The bypass switch device comprises N number of switch units and a microcontroller, wherein the microcontroller is configured to change the switching state of at least two of the N number of switch units in case of the Ethernet network module being abnormal or having malfunction, such that at least two RJ45 connectors are able to communicate with each other through the at least two switch units. In such case, the Ethernet network module is switched to work in a bypass mode. During the bypass mode, the microcontroller simultaneously controls one of two LED components included by one said RJ45 connector and one of two LED components included by another one said RJ45 connector, such that these two LED components emit lights for indicating that the Ethernet network module in in bypass mode.
TW202318849A: A computing system includes a processing unit and a network device. The processing unit includes a first baseboard management controller (BMC), an external network interface coupled to the first BMC, and a first internal network interface coupled to the first BMC. The network device includes a second BMC and a second internal network interface coupled to the second BMC. The second internal network interface of the network device is connected to the first internal network interface of the processing unit. The first BMC is configured to transfer data between an external network and the second BMC via (i) the external network interface, (ii) the first internal network interface, and (iii) the second internal network interface.
TW202335475A: The invention discloses a method for establishing a network connection between an intelligent baseboard management controller and a management server. The intelligent baseboard management controller is disposed on a motherboard of an electronic device. A connection configuration program is installed in the electronic device, and sends a connection info packet to the intelligent baseboard management controller. After receiving the connection information packet, the intelligent baseboard management controller obtains a connection information of the management server, and executes a network connection procedure according to the connection information of the management server, so that the network connection between the intelligent baseboard management controller and the management server can be established. Thus, an administrator of the management server can remotely monitor, control, or manage the electronic device by the intelligent baseboard management controller.
TWI884356B: The present disclosure describes embodiments of a device with memory and a processor. The memory is configured to store integrated circuit (IC) trim and redundancy information. The processor is configured to extract bits from the IC trim and redundancy information, perform a hashing function on the extracted bits to generate hashed bits, and in response to statistical properties of the hashed bits meeting one or more criteria, output the hashed bits. In some embodiments, the memory that stores the IC trim and redundancy information can be different from other memory used by the device for other operations (e.g., accessing user data and program data that have been written into system memory).
